ctx.name: Bill
ctx.notes: Director is not got enough asdexperience.

introQueue.label: Self-read opener
introQueue.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
introQueue.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

introQueue.label: What they're proud of
introQueue.name: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
introQueue.description: Separates real wins from performative ones; calibrates their sense of impact.

introQueue.label: Gap awareness
introQueue.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?
introQueue.description: Tests whether they can name their own gaps before the manager does.

focus_points.label: Clarity on director-level expectations
focus_points.reason: Notes mention a lack of experience. Worth clarifying if there's any uncertainty about what success looks like at this level.

selectedFocusPoints.label: Clarity on director-level expectations
selectedFocusPoints.reason: Notes mention a lack of experience. Worth clarifying if there's any uncertainty about what success looks like at this level.

brief.coreIssue: Bill, as an Acting Coach at the Director level, seems to be struggling with understanding and meeting director-level expectations. His experience might not fully align with the demands of his current role, which could be affecting his performance and confidence. The meeting should focus on clarifying what is expected from him at this senior level, identifying any gaps in skills or knowledge, and discussing strategies to bridge these gaps effectively. It's crucial to provide him with a clear roadmap so he can align his efforts with organizational goals.
brief.openingQuestion: Bill, can you walk me through your understanding of the key responsibilities and expectations at the director level in your role?
listenFor: whether he identifies specific director-level responsibilities
if they express uncertainty about expectations
whether he mentions any support needed
avoid: do not assume he knows all director-level expectations
do not overlook his potential strategic insights
brief.goodOutcome: You will have a mutual understanding of director-level expectations and a plan to address any gaps Bill identifies.
brief.suggestedAction: Agree on a follow-up meeting in two weeks to review progress and adjust the development plan as necessary.

issues: coreIssue is too long (88 words — max 80)
